 {"id":"bd-2hg1","title":"Port ConfirmDialog widget to ftui Modal","description":"# Port ConfirmDialog to ftui Modal

## What
Replace the hand-rolled ConfirmDialog (tui/widgets/confirm_dialog.rs, 361 lines) with
ftui's built-in Modal widget, which provides centered overlay, backdrop dimming, and
focus trapping out of the box.

## Current ConfirmDialog Features
- Modal overlay centered on screen
- Title: "Confirm Execution"
- Body: List of selected processes with actions
- Two buttons: [Yes] [No] with keyboard navigation (Tab/Enter/y/n)
- Dimmed background behind dialog
- Border with title

### ConfirmDialogState
```rust
pub struct ConfirmDialogState {
    visible: bool,
    selected_button: ConfirmButton,  // Yes/No
    items: Vec<String>,              // Process descriptions to confirm
}
```

## ftui Modal Widget
ftui's Modal provides:
- Centered content rendering with configurable width/height
- Backdrop with optional dimming/blur
- Focus trapping (keyboard events don't leak to background)
- Title bar
- Built-in close button (optional)
- Animation support (fade in/out)

### Usage
```rust
let modal = Modal::new()
    .title("Confirm Execution")
    .width_percent(60)
    .height_percent(40)
    .backdrop_style(Style::new().bg(Color::Rgb(Rgb(0, 0, 0))).add_modifier(Modifier::DIM));

// Inside the modal content:
// - List of processes
// - [Yes] [No] buttons
```

## Button Navigation
Current: Tab switches between Yes/No, Enter confirms selection, y/n shortcuts
In ftui: We can use Button widgets or styled Paragraph segments for [Yes] [No].
Focus management handled via FocusGraph or manual state tracking.

## Files to Modify
- `crates/pt-core/src/tui/widgets/confirm_dialog.rs` — rewrite using ftui Modal

## Acceptance Criteria
- Modal renders centered with dimmed backdrop
- Title shows "Confirm Execution"
- Body lists selected processes with planned actions
- Tab switches between Yes/No buttons
- Enter confirms selected button
- y/n keyboard shortcuts work
- Esc dismisses dialog (same as No)
- Dialog correctly overlays other content","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:11:37.322800960Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T01:42:46.702413343Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T01:42:46.702334677Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["tui","widget"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2hg1","depends_on_id":"bd-2hmx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:18:49.479678917Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2hg1","depends_on_id":"bd-3hpi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:18:41.251171837Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2hg1","depends_on_id":"bd-3qvi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:11:37.358216265Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2hmx","title":"Port theme system to ftui Theme/StyleSheet","description":"# Port Theme System to ftui with WCAG Accessibility

## What
Migrate the 3-theme system (Dark/Light/HighContrast) from ratatui Color/Style to ftui's
Theme + StyleSheet system. The current theme.rs is 212 lines.

This bead also absorbs the WCAG color accessibility requirements from process_triage-ktge
(accessibility features). Since ftui provides built-in contrast validation functions
(`best_text_color()`, `meets_wcag_aa()`, `meets_wcag_aaa()`), these requirements are
naturally implemented as part of theme construction rather than as a separate concern.

---

## Current Theme (tui/theme.rs)
```rust
pub struct Theme {
    pub bg: Color, pub fg: Color,
    pub header_bg: Color, pub header_fg: Color,
    pub selected_bg: Color, pub selected_fg: Color,
    pub border: Color,
    pub kill_fg: Color,      // Red
    pub review_fg: Color,    // Yellow
    pub spare_fg: Color,     // Green
    pub danger: Color, pub warning: Color, pub success: Color,
    pub muted: Color,
    pub search_highlight: Color,
}
impl Theme {
    pub fn dark() -> Self { ... }
    pub fn light() -> Self { ... }
    pub fn high_contrast() -> Self { ... }
}
```

## ftui Theme System
ftui provides two complementary systems:

### 1. Theme (semantic color slots)
```rust
let theme = ThemeBuilder::new("process-triage-dark")
    .slot("bg", Color::Rgb(Rgb(30, 30, 30)))
    .slot("fg", Color::Rgb(Rgb(220, 220, 220)))
    .slot("kill", Color::Rgb(Rgb(255, 80, 80)))
    .slot("review", Color::Rgb(Rgb(255, 200, 50)))
    .slot("spare", Color::Rgb(Rgb(80, 255, 80)))
    .build();
```

### 2. StyleSheet (named style classes, CSS-like)
```rust
let mut sheet = StyleSheet::new();
sheet.register("table.header", Style::new().fg(theme["header_fg"]).bold());
sheet.register("table.selected", Style::new().bg(theme["selected_bg"]));
sheet.register("classification.kill", Style::new().fg(theme["kill"]).bold());
sheet.register("classification.review", Style::new().fg(theme["review"]));
sheet.register("classification.spare", Style::new().fg(theme["spare"]));
sheet.register("search.highlight", Style::new().bg(theme["search_highlight"]));
```

### 3. TableTheme (for ftui Table widget)
ftui has built-in table themes with preset styles. We can use these or customize.

## Migration Mapping

| Current field | ftui semantic slot | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| bg/fg | "surface"/"on-surface" | Follow Material-style naming |
| header_bg/fg | "surface-variant"/"on-surface-variant" | |
| selected_bg/fg | "primary"/"on-primary" | |
| border | "outline" | |
| kill_fg | "error" or "classification.kill" | Domain-specific |
| review_fg | "warning" or "classification.review" | Domain-specific |
| spare_fg | "success" or "classification.spare" | Domain-specific |
| danger/warning/success | "error"/"warning"/"success" | Standard semantic |
| muted | "on-surface-variant" | |
| search_highlight | "tertiary" | |

---

## WCAG Accessibility (from process_triage-ktge)

### Contrast Validation
ftui provides built-in contrast validation:
```rust
use ftui::style::{best_text_color, meets_wcag_aa, meets_wcag_aaa};

fn validate_theme(theme: &Theme) -> Vec<Warning> {
    let mut warnings = Vec::new();
    // WCAG AA requires 4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text
    if \!meets_wcag_aa(theme["classification.kill"], theme["surface"]) {
        warnings.push("Kill classification color fails WCAG AA against background");
    }
    // Validate all classification colors in all themes
    for slot in ["kill", "review", "spare"] {
        for bg in ["surface", "surface-variant", "primary"] {
            assert\!(meets_wcag_aa(theme[slot], theme[bg]),
                "{slot} on {bg} fails WCAG AA");
        }
    }
    warnings
}
```

### NO_COLOR Support
Respect the `NO_COLOR` environment variable (https://no-color.org/):
```rust
if std::env::var("NO_COLOR").is_ok() {
    // Strip all color, use bold/underline/reverse for emphasis
    return Theme::no_color();
}
```

### High Contrast Requirements
The HighContrast theme must meet WCAG AAA (7:1 ratio):
- Pure white text on pure black background
- Classification colors: bright, saturated versions
- Bold text for all classification labels
- No color-only information (always pair with text/icon)
- Use ftui `meets_wcag_aaa()` to validate

### Environment Variable Detection
```rust
let theme = if std::env::var("NO_COLOR").is_ok() {
    Theme::no_color()
} else if std::env::var("PT_HIGH_CONTRAST").is_ok() {
    Theme::high_contrast()
} else {
    Theme::dark()  // default
};
```

### Color Downgrading
When terminal supports fewer colors:
- 256-color: use closest ANSI-256 approximation
- 16-color: use standard ANSI colors (bold for bright)
- No color: use text attributes only (bold, underline, reverse)

ftui handles some of this automatically, but we should test with `TERM=xterm` (16-color)
and `TERM=dumb` (no color).

---

## Unit Tests for Theme

### Test Cases (~10)
```rust
#[test]
fn dark_theme_classification_colors_meet_wcag_aa() {
    let theme = build_dark_theme();
    assert\!(meets_wcag_aa(theme.color("kill"), theme.color("surface")));
    assert\!(meets_wcag_aa(theme.color("review"), theme.color("surface")));
    assert\!(meets_wcag_aa(theme.color("spare"), theme.color("surface")));
}

#[test]
fn high_contrast_theme_meets_wcag_aaa() {
    let theme = build_high_contrast_theme();
    // WCAG AAA = 7:1 ratio
    for slot in all_foreground_slots() {
        assert\!(meets_wcag_aaa(theme.color(slot), theme.color("surface")),
            "Slot {slot} fails WCAG AAA");
    }
}

#[test]
fn no_color_theme_uses_no_rgb_colors() {
    let theme = build_no_color_theme();
    // All color slots should be Reset or terminal default
    for slot in all_color_slots() {
        assert\!(\!theme.color(slot).is_rgb(), "Slot {slot} has RGB color in no-color mode");
    }
}

#[test]
fn stylesheet_has_all_required_classes() {
    let sheet = build_stylesheet(&build_dark_theme());
    let required = ["table.header", "table.selected", "classification.kill",
                    "classification.review", "classification.spare", "search.highlight"];
    for class in required {
        assert\!(sheet.has(class), "Missing stylesheet class: {class}");
    }
}

#[test]
fn theme_switching_at_runtime() {
    let mut app = create_test_app();
    assert_eq\!(app.theme_name(), "dark");
    app.update(Msg::SwitchTheme("light".into()));
    assert_eq\!(app.theme_name(), "light");
}
```

---

## Files to Modify
- `crates/pt-core/src/tui/theme.rs` — rewrite using ftui Theme + StyleSheet + WCAG

## Acceptance Criteria
- Three themes (Dark, Light, HighContrast) defined using ftui ThemeBuilder
- StyleSheet with named classes for all UI elements
- Classification colors (KILL/REVIEW/SPARE) meet WCAG AA contrast in all themes
- HighContrast theme meets WCAG AAA (7:1)
- NO_COLOR environment variable respected
- PT_HIGH_CONTRAST environment variable supported
- Theme can be switched at runtime via Model state
- Unit tests validate contrast ratios for all color combinations
- No color-only information (all color meanings have text fallback)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:08:07.820074344Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T00:53:05.568059204Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T00:53:05.567984024Z","close_reason":"Completed: Rewrote theme.rs with ftui Theme/ThemeBuilder + StyleSheet. 4 themes (Dark, Light, HighContrast, NoColor). WCAG AA validated for all themes, AAA for HighContrast. NO_COLOR and PT_HIGH_CONTRAST env vars supported. 11 named stylesheet classes. Legacy ratatui compat retained behind ui-legacy feature. 15 unit tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["theme","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2hmx","depends_on_id":"bd-1iuh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:17:22.003345204Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2hmx","depends_on_id":"bd-3qvi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:08:07.843725770Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2jlf","title":"Verify and update BATS E2E TUI test suite for ftui migration","description":"# Verify and Update BATS E2E TUI Test Suite for ftui Migration

## What
After the ftui migration, verify that the existing 68 BATS E2E TUI workflow tests
(test/test_e2e_tui.bats) still pass against the new ftui-based binary. Update any
tests that break due to rendering differences, terminal initialization changes, or
PTY behavior changes. Also add new BATS tests for ftui-specific features.

## Background
The BATS E2E tests (added in commit 7141e22) test the actual pt-core binary via PTY
recording with JSONL logs. They are **separate** from the Rust-level E2E tests (bd-1ujr)
which test at the Message/Model level. BATS tests exercise the real binary, real terminal,
real PTY — they are the final line of defense.

## Why BATS Tests May Need Updates
1. **Terminal initialization**: ftui's terminal setup sequence differs from crossterm's
2. **ANSI escape sequences**: ftui's renderer may emit different sequences for the same visual
3. **Timing**: ftui's event loop has different timing characteristics
4. **Screen cleanup**: ftui's RAII cleanup may change exit behavior
5. **New features**: inline mode, toast notifications not covered by existing tests

---

## Verification Steps

### Step 1: Run existing BATS tests against ftui binary
```bash
bats test/test_e2e_tui.bats
```
Record which tests pass and which fail. Categorize failures:
- **Rendering diff**: Same behavior but different ANSI output
- **Timing diff**: Race condition from different event loop
- **Missing feature**: Something removed or not yet ported
- **True regression**: Behavioral difference

### Step 2: Fix rendering differences
If ftui produces different ANSI sequences for the same visual output, update
test expectations. Use `BLESS=1` pattern or update golden files.

### Step 3: Fix timing differences
ftui's event loop may process events at different rates. Adjust sleep/wait
timeouts in tests. Use event-based synchronization where possible.

### Step 4: Add ftui-specific tests
New BATS tests for features that exist in ftui but not in ratatui:
- Toast notification appears after scan complete
- Inline mode renders at bottom of terminal (if --inline flag exists)
- Theme switching via runtime command
- CommandPalette opens and closes (if implemented)
- Evidence ledger export creates file

---

## New BATS Test Cases (~8 additional)

```bash
@test "E2E TUI: ftui terminal cleanup on normal exit" {
    # Verify terminal is fully restored after quit
    run timeout 5 "$PT_CORE" run --interactive --standalone
    # Send q to quit
    # Check terminal state restored (no alt-screen, cursor visible)
}

@test "E2E TUI: ftui terminal cleanup on panic" {
    # Force a panic condition and verify terminal is restored
    # ftui RAII should handle this
}

@test "E2E TUI: toast notification after scan refresh" {
    # Send r (refresh), verify toast appears in JSONL log
}

@test "E2E TUI: theme switch persists across session" {
    # Switch theme, quit, relaunch, verify theme
}

@test "E2E TUI: evidence ledger export creates file" {
    # Navigate to Galaxy Brain, press x, verify file created
}

@test "E2E TUI: search with special characters" {
    # Enter search with regex-like chars, verify no crash
}

@test "E2E TUI: responsive layout at various terminal sizes" {
    # Test with different COLUMNS/LINES values
}

@test "E2E TUI: NO_COLOR mode produces no ANSI escapes" {
    # NO_COLOR=1 pt-core run --interactive
    # Verify no color escape sequences in output
}
```

---

## Test Infrastructure Updates
- Update `test/test_helper/common.bash` if needed for ftui-specific setup
- Ensure PTY recording captures ftui's output correctly
- JSONL logging should still work (ftui integrates with tracing)

## Files to Modify
- `test/test_e2e_tui.bats` — update failing tests, add new ftui-specific tests
- `test/test_helper/common.bash` — update if helper functions need ftui-aware changes

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 68 existing BATS TUI tests pass (updated for ftui rendering if needed)
- 8+ new BATS tests for ftui-specific features pass
- PTY recording captures ftui output correctly
- JSONL logs produced during BATS tests are valid
- Terminal cleanup verified (no leftover alt-screen)
- NO_COLOR mode tested
- Tests run in CI (bats test/test_e2e_tui.bats)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:53:03.209816639Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:53:14.684582130Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["test","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2jlf","depends_on_id":"bd-1ujr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:53:11.727551866Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2jlf","depends_on_id":"bd-342l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:53:14.684544541Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2jlf","depends_on_id":"bd-3qvi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:53:03.229686542Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2ku4","title":"Add --label flag to agent plan command","description":"## Goal
Add `--label` flag to `agent plan` command so plan sessions can be labeled at creation time.

## Background
The `diff --baseline` feature looks for sessions with label 'baseline' that have inventory + inference artifacts. Currently, only `agent snapshot` supports `--label`, but snapshot sessions lack the required artifacts (scan/inventory.json, inference/results.json). This creates a gap: the only way to label a plan session is to manually patch manifest.json after creation.

## Scope
- Add `--label` arg to `AgentPlanArgs` struct
- Pass label through to `SessionManifest::new()` call in `run_agent_plan`
- Include label in JSON output
- Update help text

## Acceptance Criteria
- `pt-core agent plan --label baseline` creates a session with label in manifest
- `diff --baseline` works with plan sessions labeled 'baseline'
- Existing tests still pass

## Discovered during
bd-1rf2 (E2E session diff tests) — had to patch manifest.json manually in tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LavenderBasin","created_at":"2026-02-06T02:45:50.782885032Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T02:50:02.396843550Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T02:50:02.396774441Z","close_reason":"Added --label flag to agent plan command. Label is persisted in manifest.json and emitted in JSON output. Two new E2E tests verify the feature. diff --baseline now works cleanly with labeled plan sessions.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2o00","title":"No-mock coverage remaining surfaces (output/capabilities/supervision/shadow)","description":"## Goal
Close remaining no-mock unit + integration coverage gaps (output/capabilities/supervision/shadow) and ensure each surface has paired E2E coverage with rich JSONL logging.

## Scope (Child Tasks)
- Output formatting + token-efficient pipeline (JSON/TOON/compact/fields) tests.
- Capabilities/environment detection (pt-common + pt-core manifest/caching) tests.
- Supervision detection + routing (systemd/launchd/container/agent) tests.
- Shadow mode capture + retention pipeline tests.
- Fixture governance: sanitized, deterministic fixtures + checksums used across tests.

## Required Test Types (per child)
- Unit: pure parser/formatter/logic coverage with real structs (no mocks).
- Integration: run real code paths end-to-end on fixtures.
- E2E: CLI-level scripts (may live in bd-3p7w children) with JSONL logs + artifact manifest.

## Logging & Artifacts
- JSONL logs per test case: event, timestamp, phase, case_id, cmd, exit_code, duration_ms, artifacts[].
- Artifacts stored under `test/artifacts/*` with checksums in manifest.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Coverage gaps are closed for all listed surfaces without mocks.

 {"id":"bd-342l","title":"Remove ratatui/crossterm dependencies and dead code","description":"# Remove ratatui/crossterm Dependencies and Dead Code

## What
Final cleanup: remove all ratatui and crossterm dependencies, imports, the `ui-legacy`
feature flag (if created during migration), and any remaining bridge/compatibility code.

## Checklist
1. Remove from `crates/pt-core/Cargo.toml`:
   - `ratatui = { version = "0.29", optional = true }`
   - `crossterm = { version = "0.28", optional = true }`
   - `ui-legacy` feature (if it exists from migration period in bd-1iuh)
2. Search and remove all `use ratatui::` imports
3. Search and remove all `use crossterm::` imports
4. Remove any `#[cfg(feature = "ui-legacy")]` blocks
5. Remove any compatibility shims, bridge types, or migration adapters
6. Update `[features]` section: `ui = ["ftui"]` (clean, no legacy refs)
7. Run `cargo build -p pt-core --features ui` — no warnings about unused deps
8. Run `cargo build -p pt-core` (without ui) — clean build
9. Check that no other crates in the workspace depend on ratatui/crossterm
10. Run Cargo.lock update to prune removed dependencies

## Verification
```bash
# No ratatui references should remain
rg "ratatui" crates/pt-core/src/ --type rust
# Should return nothing

# No crossterm references should remain
rg "crossterm" crates/pt-core/src/ --type rust
# Should return nothing

# No legacy feature gate references
rg "ui-legacy" crates/pt-core/
# Should return nothing

# Clean build
cargo build -p pt-core --features ui
cargo test -p pt-core --features ui

# Clean Cargo.lock
cargo update -p pt-core
```

## Structured Logging
```rust
// Before removal, log what's being cleaned
tracing::info!(
    target: "migration.cleanup",
    removed_deps = ?["ratatui", "crossterm"],
    "Legacy TUI dependencies removed"
);
```

## Files to Modify
- `crates/pt-core/Cargo.toml` — remove deps, clean features
- `Cargo.lock` — prune removed dependencies
- Any remaining .rs files with old imports or legacy feature gates

## Acceptance Criteria
- Zero references to ratatui or crossterm in pt-core source
- Zero references to `ui-legacy` feature anywhere
- Clean build with and without `ui` feature
- All E2E tests pass (bd-1ujr proved this)
- No unused dependency warnings
- Cargo.lock pruned of removed dependencies","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:15:07.703289886Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T16:27:11.825582645Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T16:27:11.825455488Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cleanup","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-342l","depends_on_id":"bd-1ujr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:21:56.414756426Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-342l","depends_on_id":"bd-3qvi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:15:07.727417714Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-34f6","title":"Add Toast notifications for async operation feedback","description":"# Add Toast Notifications

## What
Use ftui's Toast/NotificationQueue widgets to provide transient feedback for async
operations (scan complete, execution complete, errors) instead of status bar messages.

## Current Feedback
Status bar shows "Refreshing..." → "Scan complete (42 processes)". This is easy to miss.

## Toast Upgrade
```rust
// After scan completes:
self.notifications.push(Toast::new("Scan complete")
    .body("42 processes found, 3 recommended for action")
    .kind(ToastKind::Success)
    .duration(Duration::from_secs(3)));

// After execution:
self.notifications.push(Toast::new("Execution complete")
    .body("2 processes terminated")
    .kind(ToastKind::Info)
    .duration(Duration::from_secs(5)));

// On error:
self.notifications.push(Toast::new("Scan failed")
    .body("Permission denied: /proc/1234/status")
    .kind(ToastKind::Error)
    .duration(Duration::from_secs(10)));
```

## Rendering
Toast widgets render as overlays in a corner (top-right or bottom-right).
ftui's NotificationQueue manages stacking and auto-dismiss timing.

## Files to Modify
- `crates/pt-core/src/tui/app.rs` — NotificationQueue state, rendering in view()
- Update() handlers for scan/execute results

## Acceptance Criteria
- Success toast on scan complete
- Info toast on execution complete
- Error toast on failures
- Toasts auto-dismiss after configured duration
- Multiple toasts stack correctly","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:16:10.197308273Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:22:44.640098748Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["enhancement","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-34f6","depends_on_id":"bd-3hpi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:22:44.640063713Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-34f6","depends_on_id":"bd-3qvi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:16:10.253698326Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-35oy","title":"Fix inference test failures: default priors missing command_categories","description":"## Summary
Two tests in interpretation_examples.rs are failing because the default priors don't include command_categories Dirichlet distributions:

1. example_1_bun_test_stalled_signals_shift_posterior - Got abandoned=0.000 vs useful=0.051
2. example_4_claude_process_stalled - Got abandoned=0.367 (expected >0.5)

## Root Cause
The zombie class has extreme priors for boolean features (orphan_beta, tty_beta, net_beta, io_active_beta) that are absorbing probability mass that should go to abandoned. Without command_categories, the model cannot distinguish between abandoned agent and actual zombie process.

## Evidence
- priors.default.json has no command_categories section
- Tests pass command_category evidence (CommandCategory::Agent, CommandCategory::Test)
- log_lik_dirichlet returns 0.0 when command_categories is None

## Proposed Fix
Add command_categories to priors.default.json with Dirichlet alphas that disfavor zombie class for Agent/Test/Server categories.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-22T08:05:14.684642120Z","created_by":"TealGorge","updated_at":"2026-01-22T08:12:21.200658743Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T08:12:21.200606324Z","close_reason":"fixed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-36a2","title":"Port TUI golden, snapshot, and integration tests to ftui-harness","description":"# Port TUI Golden, Snapshot, and Integration Tests to ftui-harness

## What
Migrate **both** the TUI golden tests (crates/pt-core/tests/tui_golden.rs, ~100 lines) **and** the TUI integration tests (crates/pt-core/tests/tui_integration.rs, ~83 lines) from ratatui TestBackend/Buffer assertions to ftui-harness snapshot testing and ftui Model-based assertions.

This bead consolidates what were previously two separate test beads (golden tests + integration tests) because both share test infrastructure, both depend on the same widget implementations being complete, and combining them enables better test organization: golden/snapshot tests validate visual output, integration tests validate state transitions and interaction flows.

---

## Part A: Golden/Snapshot Tests (formerly standalone bead)

### Current Golden Tests
- Render ProcessDetail widget with sample data → assert buffer contents
- Render ProcessTable with sample rows → assert column layout
- Render Galaxy Brain view → assert mathematical output format
- Uses ratatui::backend::TestBackend with fixed dimensions

### ftui-harness Snapshot Framework
ftui provides `ftui-harness` crate with:
- `TestTerminal` — in-memory terminal with buffer capture
- Snapshot comparison with ANSI output
- `BLESS=1` to update baselines
- Deterministic rendering (same input = same output, guaranteed by ftui proofs)

### Usage Pattern
```rust
use ftui_harness::TestTerminal;

#[test]
fn process_table_renders_correctly() {
    let mut term = TestTerminal::new(120, 40);
    let model = create_test_model_with_rows();
    term.render(|frame| model.view(frame));
    term.assert_snapshot("process_table_default");
}
```

### Migration Strategy
1. Replace ratatui TestBackend with ftui-harness TestTerminal
2. Create sample data fixtures (ProcessRow instances with known values)
3. Render Model::view() into TestTerminal
4. Generate initial baseline snapshots with `BLESS=1 cargo test`
5. Future test runs compare against baselines

### Snapshot Test Cases (~12)
- ProcessTable default layout (120x40)
- ProcessTable compact layout (80x24)
- ProcessDetail Summary view with classification badge
- ProcessDetail Genealogy tree view
- ProcessDetail Galaxy Brain mathematical trace
- SearchInput with active query
- ConfirmDialog with buttons
- Help overlay
- StatusBar with scan info
- ConfigEditor form fields
- Dark theme rendering
- High contrast theme rendering

### Snapshot Files
Store snapshots in `crates/pt-core/tests/snapshots/`. ftui-harness stores them as `.snapshot` files with ANSI content.

---

## Part B: Integration Tests (absorbed from bd-1gut)

### Current Integration Tests
- Galaxy brain split view rendering
- Help overlay rendering
- App-level state transitions
- Uses ratatui::Terminal<TestBackend> for rendering

### ftui Model Integration Test Pattern
In ftui, integration tests can:
1. Create a Model instance
2. Send Messages via update()
3. Assert state changes
4. Render into TestTerminal and assert visual output

```rust
#[test]
fn help_overlay_shows_on_question_mark() {
    let mut app = create_test_app();
    assert_eq\!(app.state, AppState::Normal);
    
    app.update(Msg::ToggleHelp);
    assert_eq\!(app.state, AppState::Help);
    
    let mut term = TestTerminal::new(120, 40);
    term.render(|frame| app.view(frame));
    // Assert help content is visible in buffer
}
```

### Integration Test Cases (~15)
- Help overlay toggle: Normal → Help → Normal
- Search mode transitions: Normal → Searching → Normal
- Confirm dialog lifecycle: Normal → Confirming → Execute/Cancel
- Detail pane toggle shows/hides
- Detail view switching (Summary → Genealogy → Galaxy Brain)
- Theme switching preserves layout structure
- Responsive layout at 4 breakpoints (Wide/Standard/Compact/Minimal)
- Selection state persists across filter changes
- Sort toggle reverses order
- Refresh message replaces rows without losing selection
- Focus cycling via Tab/Shift+Tab
- Execute flow: select → confirm → execute → outcome display
- Empty process list renders gracefully
- Terminal too small shows minimal view or warning
- Concurrent messages process correctly (rapid key presses)

### Key Difference from E2E Tests (bd-1ujr)
E2E tests (bd-1ujr) test the full 68 workflow scenarios at the Message level with
complete behavioral coverage. These integration tests focus on **visual correctness**
of state transitions (rendering output after state changes) and **ftui Model contract**
compliance.

---

## Files to Modify
- `crates/pt-core/tests/tui_golden.rs` — rewrite using ftui-harness snapshot assertions
- `crates/pt-core/tests/tui_integration.rs` — rewrite for ftui Model + TestTerminal
- `crates/pt-core/Cargo.toml` — add ftui-harness to dev-dependencies
- `crates/pt-core/tests/snapshots/` — generated snapshot baseline files

## Test Helpers (shared by both files)
```rust
fn create_test_app() -> App { ... }
fn create_test_app_with_rows(n: usize) -> App { ... }
fn sample_rows() -> Vec<ProcessRow> { ... }
fn render_to_snapshot(app: &App, width: u16, height: u16) -> TestTerminal { ... }
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All existing golden test scenarios covered via ftui-harness snapshots
- All existing integration test scenarios covered via ftui Model assertions
- Snapshots generated and committed to repo
- Tests pass on CI (deterministic rendering guaranteed by ftui)
- `BLESS=1` workflow documented for updating baselines
- Integration tests verify both state AND visual output
- Shared test helpers reduce boilerplate
- Both test files compile with `cargo test -p pt-core --features ui`","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:14:22.870868383Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T15:56:24.019586501Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["test","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-36a2","depends_on_id":"bd-2b3l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:49:10.821563392Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-36a2","depends_on_id":"bd-2hg1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:32:09.024125239Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-36a2","depends_on_id":"bd-2ub8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:32:07.150017087Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-36a2","depends_on_id":"bd-3hfz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:21:10.285603297Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-36a2","depends_on_id":"bd-3p52","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:21:18.350869611Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-36a2","depends_on_id":"bd-3qvi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:14:22.895382090Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-38ts","title":"Perf smoke: memory/RSS sanity for large synthetic runs","description":"## Goal
Catch obvious memory regressions (RSS/alloc blowups) for large synthetic inventories.

## Scope
- Add a lightweight memory smoke check that runs a deterministic workload (e.g., build N=10k synthetic processes + inferences + diff) and samples RSS at start/end or peak (best-effort).
- Gate with very generous thresholds to avoid CI flake; goal is to catch catastrophic leaks/regressions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Runs in CI quickly and deterministically.
- Fails with a clear message when memory exceeds budget.
- Produces artifacts/logs useful for debugging (inputs size, measured RSS).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SapphireSpring","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:07:03.504072733Z","created_by":"SapphireSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-06T03:31:03.187007715Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T03:31:03.186945389Z","close_reason":"Implemented Linux-only RSS memory smoke test + CI step (commit adaf072)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bench","memory","perf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-38ts","depends_on_id":"process_triage-5g8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:07:03.585709950Z","created_by":"SapphireSpring"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3clf","title":"Add agent export command as alias for bundle create","description":"## Task
Add `pt agent export` command that acts as an ergonomic alias for `pt bundle create`.

## Background
The CLI specification (CLI_SPECIFICATION.md:419) defines `pt agent export` but implementation only has top-level `bundle create`. For agent ergonomics, the export command should be accessible under the agent subcommand.

## Bundle Format Specification
The `.ptb` (Process Triage Bundle) format is a **ZIP archive** containing:
```
session.ptb (ZIP file)
├── manifest.json         # Bundle metadata, version, creation time
├── session.json          # Session state and configuration
├── plan.json            # Generated plan (if exists)
├── actions.jsonl        # Applied actions log
├── verifications.jsonl  # Verification results
├── snapshots/           # Process snapshots (optional)
│   ├── baseline.json
│   └── post_apply.json
└── telemetry/           # Optional telemetry data
    └── events.jsonl
```

### Why ZIP?
- Universal tooling support (unzip, 7z, built-in OS support)
- Efficient compression for JSON data
- Single-file distribution
- Easy to inspect contents manually

## Implementation

### AgentExportArgs struct
```rust
/// Export session bundle (alias for bundle create)
Export(AgentExportArgs),

#[derive(Args, Debug)]
struct AgentExportArgs {
    /// Session to export (default: latest)
    #[arg(long)]
    session: Option<String>,
    
    /// Output path for bundle (REQUIRED)
    #[arg(long)]
    out: PathBuf,
    
    /// Redaction profile: minimal, safe, forensic (default: safe)
    #[arg(long, default_value = "safe")]
    profile: String,
    
    /// Include telemetry data in bundle
    #[arg(long)]
    include_telemetry: bool,
    
    /// Include memory dumps (if captured)
    #[arg(long)]
    include_dumps: bool,
    
    /// Encrypt bundle with passphrase
    #[arg(long)]
    encrypt: bool,
    
    /// Passphrase for encryption (prompted if --encrypt used without this)
    #[arg(long)]
    passphrase: Option<String>,
}
```

### Redaction Profiles
| Profile | What is included | Use case |
|---------|-----------------|----------|
| `minimal` | Hashed PIDs, no commands, no paths | Maximum privacy |
| `safe` | Redacted paths, normalized commands | Normal sharing (DEFAULT) |
| `forensic` | Full detail, no redaction | Debugging/support |

### run_agent_export function
```rust
fn run_agent_export(global: &GlobalOpts, args: &AgentExportArgs) -> ExitCode {
    // Validate profile
    if !["minimal", "safe", "forensic"].contains(&args.profile.as_str()) {
        return error_output(format!("Invalid profile: {}. Use minimal, safe, or forensic", args.profile));
    }
    
    // Delegate to bundle create logic
    run_bundle_create(
        global,
        &args.session,
        &args.out,
        &args.profile,
        args.include_telemetry,
        args.include_dumps,
        args.encrypt,
        &args.passphrase,
    )
}
```

### Output Path Handling
- If `--out` ends with `.ptb`, use as-is
- Otherwise, append `.ptb` extension
- If path exists, error unless overwriting is explicitly enabled (future flag)
- Parent directory must exist

## CLI Surface
```
pt agent export --session <id> --out <path> [OPTIONS]

Options:
  --session <id>           Session to export (default: latest)
  --out <path>             Output path for bundle (REQUIRED)
  --profile <level>        Redaction level: minimal|safe|forensic [default: safe]
  --include-telemetry      Include telemetry data
  --include-dumps          Include memory dumps
  --encrypt                Encrypt bundle
  --passphrase <phrase>    Encryption passphrase (prompted if not provided)
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `pt agent export --help` shows correct usage
- [ ] `pt agent export --session X --out Y` creates bundle
- [ ] Bundle is valid ZIP archive containing manifest.json
- [ ] All bundle create options are accessible
- [ ] Help text mentions this is equivalent to `bundle create`
- [ ] Default profile is "safe"
- [ ] --out is required (error if not provided)
- [ ] Invalid profile values return clear error
- [ ] .ptb extension is appended if missing

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests

#[test]
fn test_export_requires_out_path() {
    // --out is required, error without it
}

#[test]
fn test_export_default_profile_is_safe() {
    // Profile defaults to "safe"
}

#[test]
fn test_export_invalid_profile_rejected() {
    // --profile invalid returns error
}

#[test]

    // --out foo becomes foo.ptb
}

#[test]
fn test_export_delegates_to_bundle_create() {
    // Verify export calls bundle create logic
}
```

### Integration Tests

    // Bundle is valid ZIP file
    // Contains required files: manifest.json, session.json
}

#[test]

    // Export with --encrypt --passphrase
    // Verify bundle header indicates encryption
}

#[test]

    // Export with --profile minimal
    // Verify bundle has minimal data (no raw commands)
}
```

### E2E Test Script (test/e2e_agent_export.sh)
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# E2E test for agent export command

set -euo pipefail
LOG="test/logs/e2e_agent_export_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).log"
mkdir -p test/logs

log() { echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] $*" | tee -a "$LOG"; }

log "=== E2E Test: agent export ==="

TMPDIR=$(mktemp -d)
trap "rm -rf $TMPDIR" EXIT

# Create a session to export
log "Creating test session..."
SESSION=$(pt-core agent plan --format json | jq -r .session_id)
log "Created session: $SESSION"

# Test basic export
log "Testing basic export..."
pt-core agent export --session "$SESSION" --out "$TMPDIR/test.ptb"
if [ -f "$TMPDIR/test.ptb" ]; then
    log "✓ Bundle created at $TMPDIR/test.ptb"
    log "  Size: $(stat -c%s "$TMPDIR/test.ptb" 2>/dev/null || stat -f%z "$TMPDIR/test.ptb") bytes"
else
    log "✗ Bundle not created"
    exit 1
fi

# Verify bundle is valid ZIP (the .ptb format is ZIP)
log "Verifying bundle is valid ZIP..."
if file "$TMPDIR/test.ptb" | grep -qi "zip"; then
    log "✓ Bundle is valid ZIP archive"
elif unzip -t "$TMPDIR/test.ptb" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "✓ Bundle passes ZIP integrity check"
else
    log "✗ Bundle is not a valid ZIP"
    exit 1
fi

# Verify bundle contains required files
log "Verifying bundle structure..."
CONTENTS=$(unzip -l "$TMPDIR/test.ptb" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
if echo "$CONTENTS" | grep -q "manifest.json"; then
    log "✓ Bundle contains manifest.json"
else
    log "✗ Bundle missing manifest.json"
    echo "$CONTENTS" >> "$LOG"
    exit 1
fi

# Test --out is required
log "Testing --out is required..."
set +e
OUTPUT=$(pt-core agent export --session "$SESSION" 2>&1)
EXIT_CODE=$?
set -e
if [ "$EXIT_CODE" -ne 0 ] && echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -qi "required\|missing"; then
    log "✓ --out required error shown"
else
    log "✗ Should error without --out"
    exit 1
fi

# Test invalid profile
log "Testing invalid profile..."
set +e
OUTPUT=$(pt-core agent export --session "$SESSION" --out "$TMPDIR/bad.ptb" --profile invalid 2>&1)
EXIT_CODE=$?
set -e
if [ "$EXIT_CODE" -ne 0 ]; then
    log "✓ Invalid profile rejected"
else
    log "✗ Invalid profile should be rejected"
    exit 1
fi

# Test profile options
for PROFILE in minimal safe forensic; do
    log "Testing --profile $PROFILE..."
    pt-core agent export --session "$SESSION" --out "$TMPDIR/test_$PROFILE.ptb" --profile "$PROFILE"
    if [ -f "$TMPDIR/test_$PROFILE.ptb" ]; then
        log "✓ Profile $PROFILE works"
    else
        log "✗ Profile $PROFILE failed"
        exit 1
    fi
done

# Test encryption
log "Testing encryption..."
pt-core agent export --session "$SESSION" --out "$TMPDIR/encrypted.ptb" --encrypt --passphrase "testpass123"
if [ -f "$TMPDIR/encrypted.ptb" ]; then
    log "✓ Encrypted bundle created"
    # Encrypted bundles should not be unzippable without decryption
    if unzip -t "$TMPDIR/encrypted.ptb" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        log "⚠ Encrypted bundle appears unzippable (may use ZIP encryption)"
    else
        log "✓ Encrypted bundle requires decryption"
    fi
else
    log "✗ Encrypted bundle not created"
    exit 1
fi

# Test extension appending
log "Testing extension appending..."
pt-core agent export --session "$SESSION" --out "$TMPDIR/no_extension"
if [ -f "$TMPDIR/no_extension.ptb" ]; then
    log "✓ .ptb extension appended automatically"
elif [ -f "$TMPDIR/no_extension" ]; then
    log "⚠ Extension not appended (acceptable if documented)"
else
    log "✗ Export with no extension failed"
    exit 1
fi

log "=== E2E Test PASSED ==="
